summary: Behavior-neutral repo-wide scene nomenclature cleanup. The PR replaced ambiguous Major/Minor scene terminology with GameMode/GameScene naming across gm headers, structs, enum constants, scene routing state, data table symbols, call sites, and table-typer tooling. The stated goal was to clean up ambiguous definitions of "Minor" and "Major"; decomp.dev reported GALE01 "No changes," supporting that this was a matching-preserving rename/refactor.

key_files.why: Core enum and forward-declaration rename: MajorSceneKind/MJ_* to GameModeKind/GM_*, MinorSceneKind/MN_* to GameSceneKind/GS_*.
Struct and routing state rename: MajorScene to GameMode, MinorScene to GameScene, SceneNums to GameRouting, nums/nums2 to routing/backup.
Central scene/mode routing implementation updated to the new names, including gm_SetScene and gm_SetPendingScene.
Public prototypes and comments changed from major/minor scene terminology to mode/scene terminology.
Large scene table and transition call-site rename from MinorScene/MN_* to GameScene/GS_*.
Mode/scene data tables updated to use the new GameMode/GameScene naming.
Global symbol labels renamed, including gm_SetSceneMinor to gm_SetScene and *_MinorScenes to *_Scenes, while addresses/sizes stayed the same.
Tooling struct type registry updated from MajorScene/MinorScene/MinorSceneHandler to GameMode/GameScene/GameSceneHandler.
Generated naming heuristics updated so GameScene tables use Prep/Decide labels.

what_changed: Renamed `MajorSceneKind` to `GameModeKind` and changed enum prefixes from `MJ_*` to `GM_*` without changing ordinal values.
Renamed `MinorSceneKind` to `GameSceneKind` and changed enum prefixes from `MN_*` to `GS_*` without changing ordinal values.
Renamed scene table structs and typedefs: `MajorScene` to `GameMode`, `MinorScene` to `GameScene`, `MinorSceneHandler` to `GameSceneHandler`, and `MinorSceneInfo` to `GameSceneInfo`.

Renamed mode/scene routing fields in `GameState`: `SceneNums` became `GameRouting`; `curr_major`, `pending_major`, `prev_major` became `curr_mode`, `pending_mode`, `prev_mode`; `curr_minor`, `prev_minor`, `pending_minor` became `curr_scene`, `prev_scene`, `pending_scene`; `nums2` became `backup`.

Renamed transition APIs and internal helpers: `gm_SetSceneMinor` to `gm_SetScene`, `gm_SetScenePendingMinor` to `gm_SetPendingScene`, `matchMinor` to `nextScene`, `getScene` to `findScene`, and `findSceneMatching` to `findMode`.
Renamed data symbols from `*_MinorScenes` to `*_Scenes` in source and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, preserving existing symbol addresses and sizes.
Updated call sites across 79 changed files, including gm modules, soundtest/textlib, lbdvd, mn menu files, toy code, and headers.
Updated `tools/table-typer` so generated/typed table output recognizes `GameMode`, `GameScene`, and `GameSceneHandler` instead of the old names.
Added documentation notes in `types.h` that `GameScene` is colloquially known as "Minor Scene" and `GameMode` as "Major Scene".
smart_moves: The rename was applied consistently across headers, source files, symbol maps, and tooling, reducing the risk of stale old terminology being regenerated later.
The PR preserved binary behavior: enum values, struct layouts, symbol addresses, and function sizes remained semantically unchanged, and the decomp.dev GALE01 report said "No changes."
Adding `@note` comments for the colloquial Major/Minor terms made the new terminology searchable while acknowledging existing community language.
Renaming `nums` to `routing` and `nums2` to `backup` made the state machine intent clearer than merely replacing major/minor words mechanically.
Updating table-typer at the same time was important because otherwise future table typing could reintroduce `MinorScene` signatures or labels.
decomp_lessons: Large nomenclature refactors should include symbol files, generated/tooling assumptions, static headers, prototypes, comments, and all call sites, not only C definitions.

When renaming matched code, keep enum ordinals, struct field order, static asserts, table sizes, and symbol addresses stable; use the project matching report as evidence that the change is behavior-neutral.
If community terminology conflicts with clearer source terminology, preserve the old terms in comments or notes so future contributors can bridge both vocabularies.
Prefer semantic names that describe system roles: former major scenes are game modes, former minor scenes are game scenes, and current/pending/previous state belongs to routing.
Tooling that knows C struct names must be updated in the same PR as type renames, or later generated signatures can drift from the codebase.
Public symbol renames such as `gm_SetSceneMinor` to `gm_SetScene` need synchronized updates in C declarations, definitions, call sites, and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.
naming_conventions: Data symbols using `_MinorScenes` suffix now use `_Scenes`, e.g. `gm_803DD6A0_MinorScenes` -> `gm_803DD6A0_Scenes`.
Use `mode` for the former major-scene concept and `scene` for the former minor-scene concept; keep "Major Scene" and "Minor Scene" only as colloquial documentation when useful.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-specific matching trick is evident; this was a mechanical naming cleanup.
The decomp.dev bot reported `Report for GALE01 (0030f76 - eb19533): No changes`, indicating the rename preserved the built binary output.
The symbol map changed labels only: addresses, scopes, sizes, and object/function classifications in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` stayed aligned.
The diff preserved enum numeric comments and table sentinel patterns, avoiding layout or control-flow changes while renaming constants.
Updating table-typer function pointer signatures from `MinorScene*` to `GameScene*` prevented generated C from creating type mismatches after the rename.

review_feedback.feedback: Approved with "Looks good to me" while deferring to r-burns or PsiLupan.
LGTM, but noted the community has settled on the Major/Minor terminology and that the difference will need to be reconciled eventually.

follow_up_queries: GameModeKind GM_* GameSceneKind GS_* MajorScene MinorScene
GameRouting nums nums2 backup curr_mode curr_scene
